title: rubus lepidulus
intro: Rubus lepidulus, also known as the prickly raspberry or dwarf thimbleberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae). It is native to North America and is known for its edible fruits, which have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The fruits of Rubus lepidulus have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems and skin irritations, and have also been used to promote wound healing and reduce pain. Herbal preparations of Rubus lepidulus include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves, stems, and fruits of the plant in hot water or a solvent.
